summary: Tested Autonomy Engine Phase A1+A2 (backend + frontend). All 11 backend pytest cases pass: /score, /history, /snapshot, /targets GET+PUT including weight normalization, cache invalidation, and 403 for non-admin. Frontend /admin/autonomy renders with Romanian copy, score ring, tier badge, 5 sub-score cards, drill-down modal open/close for all 5 sub-scores (operational/technical/security/dev/ai), Refresh + Snapshot acum buttons functional, and 'Autonomy Engine' sidebar entry visible. No ObjectId serialization errors. No regression on /api/admin/ai-control/overview and /api/admin/healthcheck/run.
critical_code_review_comments: engine.py: _score_ai uses `await db.list_collection_names()` twice (one per check) — minor perf; could be memoized once per compute call.

engine.py: queries against `created_at`/`started_at`/`timestamp` use `.isoformat()` string comparison. Works because ISO-8601 is lexicographically sortable, but only safe if all stored timestamps are stored as ISO strings with timezone. Mixed datetime vs string storage would silently break the count. Recommend documenting/enforcing string ISO format in those collections.
autonomy.py /history: uses {'timestamp': {'$gte': since.isoformat()}} — same concern as above; consistent with snapshot writer which stores report['computed_at'] (ISO string), so OK in isolation.

autonomy.py take_autonomy_snapshot: cleanup query `find({}, {_id:1}).sort('timestamp',-1).skip(400)` will scan the full collection; once capped fine, but consider TTL index on timestamp for very large fleets.

autonomy.py update_targets: only normalizes weights when provided; weights dict received from PUT may contain a subset of keys and still divide by partial sum — current frontend always sends all 5, but server should validate the 5 expected keys are present and complete to avoid silent drift.
Test endpoint discrepancy: review_request mentioned /api/admin/healthcheck but the actual route is /api/admin/healthcheck/run (no top-level GET). Not a bug, just clarification.
seed_data_creation: Forced one POST /api/admin/autonomy/snapshot which inserted a doc into autonomy_snapshots (history count went from N to N+1). Also temporarily overwrote autonomy_targets config then restored originals in the same test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Autonomy Engine is fully working end-to-end. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_autonomy_engine.py and run in ~3s. All 5 data-testid sub-score cards open drill-down modal; data-testid coverage is complete on the page (autonomy-title, autonomy-tier, autonomy-ring, autonomy-back, autonomy-refresh, autonomy-take-snap, autonomy-sub-*, autonomy-drilldown, autonomy-drilldown-close). APScheduler job 'autonomy_snapshot_daily' is registered in server.py (verified via code review at lines 195-201); no explicit startup log line for that specific job, but other scheduler jobs log fine. Tier was 'Manual' during test because env has low signals (score=27) — that's expected for a fresh dev env.
